tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto for early trip shopping
tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'eyeglasses Kyoto'
tasks.hook_line: You're already in Japan—Kyoto is next and glasses shopping needs to happen early.
tasks.reward: Gets glasses shopping on the route so you can snag 4 pairs without backtracking
nuggets: Kyoto has Jins, Zoff, Owndays chains
tasks.system_prompt: I'm in Japan right now (Kyoto is my next stop) and need to buy 4 pairs of eyeglasses during this trip. Help me find eyeglasses stores in Kyoto that are convenient to reach from central Kyoto hotels. I want a mix of affordable chain stores (Jins, Zoff, Owndays) and any interesting independent opticians. Give me store names, addresses, Google Maps links, opening hours, and notes on selection/price ranges. I need to knock this out early in my Kyoto stay (March 25-27) so I'm not carrying glasses around for the rest of the trip.

tasks.task_name: Validate insurance/prescription requirements for buying 4 pairs of glasses
tasks.first_domino: Email insurance agent: subject 'Japan glasses coverage'
tasks.hook_line: You're day 11—better confirm coverage before dropping $400+ on glasses.
tasks.reward: Removes the 'what if insurance won't cover this' worry before you buy
nuggets: Most US vision insurance covers abroad
tasks.system_prompt: I'm in Japan and planning to buy 4 pairs of eyeglasses during this trip. I have US vision insurance but I'm not sure what the requirements are for international purchases. Help me draft a quick email to my insurance agent asking: (1) Does my plan cover eyeglasses purchased in Japan? (2) What documentation do I need to submit for reimbursement? (3) Any prescription requirements or limits? Also, confirm whether my current US prescription is valid for purchasing glasses abroad or if I need an eye exam in Japan first.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'Kyoto temples'
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has 2000+ temples—which ones are actually worth the early wake-up?
tasks.reward: Locks in a confident Kyoto plan so you can just execute on arrival
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 2.5 days in Kyoto (March 25-27) for me and my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, walking, outdoor spaces, nature, and good food. We've already got hotels in central Kyoto. I want a day-by-day itinerary with opening hours, Google Maps links, and walking routes. Mix major landmarks (Fushimi Inari, Kinkaku-ji, Arashiyama) with less touristy spots. Include timing advice (when to go to beat crowds) and food recommendations near each area. We're comfortable walking 10+ miles a day.

tasks.hook_line: Takayama is mountain town Japan—old streets, sake breweries, and Alps access.
tasks.reward: Defines Takayama days so Tokyo is the only blank left
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for Takayama, Japan (staying 2 nights, roughly March 28-29). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like nature, walking, local food, and cultural experiences. Takayama is known for old town streets, morning markets, and access to the Japanese Alps. I want day-by-day suggestions with timing, Google Maps links, and any day-trip options (like Shirakawa-go village). Include food and sake brewery recommendations. We're open to light hiking if weather permits.

tasks.hook_line: Tokyo is huge—which neighborhoods match your vibe vs tourist traps?
tasks.reward: Locks in Tokyo plan—the last big piece of the trip
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 5-6 days in Tokyo (roughly March 30 - April 6) for me and my girlfriend Dana. We like exploring neighborhoods, street food, quirky experiences, parks, and shopping (especially stationery, fountain pens, watches). We're less interested in anime/gaming. I want a neighborhood-by-neighborhood guide: which areas to prioritize, what to do in each, best times to visit, food spots, and Google Maps links. Include day-trip options like Mount Fuji or Nikko. We walk a lot and are comfortable navigating public transit.

tasks.first_domino: Clone the Cerebro repo and open README
tasks.hook_line: Can you get this developer portal stack running without hitting dependency hell?
tasks.reward: Unblocks all Cerebro work—you can finally contribute to the project
tasks.system_prompt: I'm trying to get the Cerebro project (a developer portal for engineers) running on my local machine. I haven't touched it since November 2025. Walk me through the setup process step-by-step: (1) What dependencies do I need? (2) What's the typical local dev setup for a developer portal? (3) What are common gotchas when running these projects locally? (4) How do I verify it's working? Assume I'm on macOS, comfortable with terminal, and have Docker/Node.js available. Give me a troubleshooting checklist if things break.

recommended_slots.reason: Urgency + low activation energy—you're in Japan now and Kyoto is next, research is quick.
Unblocks glasses shopping with minimal effort—just an email to insurance agent.
High-value planning for Kyoto arrival, matches ramp_up energy for medium-effort research.
